rationale: Series-coherence / gate-forwardness lens clears on all five checks. (a) 110/111 boundary is clean: R6 resolvers, R7 recorded field + ApproveThreshold() interpreter, R8 ReviewerCountNote/PanelGateDecision, R9 config show are each landable and testable standalone (acceptance criteria construct Panel structs and call renderConfig directly; no panel.json WRITER needed). internal/panel imports 0 of internal/config today (verified go list -deps). (b) Verified against OWNERSHIP.yaml: internal/config -> core, internal/panel + cmd/** -> workflow; per ADR-0032 tri-state, an Accepted ADR-0040 with Domain(s): core, workflow yields coveredAccepted for every source bead at complete-time. (c) Per-bead doc-sync stated (core bead -> core doc, workflow bead -> workflow doc; ADR edits doc-classified and self-carrying; Bead 1 ADR lands first). (d) Natural 4-bead one-commit split (ADR / config / panel / cmd), within 3-5. (e) 108 source packages (approve/validate/doctor/adr/trace/contextpack) are disjoint from 109 (config/panel/new-cmd); 108 leaves cmd/** unchanged, 109 doesn't touch OWNERSHIP.yaml. Only advisory nits below; none block approval.
findings: ADVISORY (Goal wording): Goal 2 states the panel: block 'is wired into the panel machinery as the creation-time default for a fresh panel.json' — read literally this promises a 109 deliverable (a writer/creation path) that Requirements R6, Scope In ('lands the resolvers and the recorded field the 110 writer will call'), Scope Out, and the Open Questions all explicitly defer to spec 110. Every authoritative section is crisp and consistent; only the Goal summary overstates. Suggest rewording to 'provides the creation-time-default resolvers the spec-110 writer will consume' so the 109/110 boundary the three-spec split protects reads cleanly in the Goal too.

ADVISORY (plan-time, not spec): the doc-sync constraint gives both workflow beads (panel bead R7/R8, cmd bead R9) the same target directory .mindspec/domains/workflow/*.md; the spec doesn't pin bead-UNIQUE doc files. Sequential merges are fine, but the plan should give the two workflow beads distinct workflow doc files (or a stable ordering) to avoid same-file churn. Similarly, 109's workflow-doc edits may overlap in-flight spec 108's workflow-doc edits — a rebase-resolvable doc overlap, not a code conflict (source packages remain disjoint).

VERIFIED: ADR-0040 Domain(s): core, workflow covers both impacted domains at complete-time; internal/panel stays a config-free leaf; ADR/domain-doc edits are doc-classified and don't trip adr-divergence-unowned; threshold-0 loophole closed on both config side (R5) and record side (R7).
